data.brief_md:
# INCIDENT: Emotional-support llama in us-east-1

`scenario_tag: llama`

At 09:14 PT, Biscuit — the CEO's emotional-support llama — breached the badge gate of our Ashburn datacenter, chewed through a cable tray, and took region us-east-1 offline for 43 minutes. A staff engineer's livestream of the pursuit has 2.1M views. Customers are furious; the llama is fine.

issues.title: Physical security failure: a llama defeated the badge gate
Livestream of the pursuit is at 2.1M views and climbing

briefs.fact_checker: Verify outage duration, SLA terms, and whether Biscuit is actually authorized on-site.
briefs.legal: Assess SLA breach exposure and flag any liability-admitting language.
briefs.comms: Draft empathy-first key messages; customers first, llama second.
briefs.social: Assess the meme situation; the internet loves Biscuit and hates our uptime.

data.brief: Verify outage duration, SLA terms, and whether Biscuit is actually authorized on-site.
Assess SLA breach exposure and flag any liability-admitting language.
Draft empathy-first key messages; customers first, llama second.
Assess the meme situation; the internet loves Biscuit and hates our uptime.

data.text: Checking the claimed outage duration against our incident records...

data.summary: INC-104: prior 12-min us-east-1 blip in March; we promised 'multiple redundant safeguards'...

data.text: Reviewing SLA breach precedents and scanning for banned phrases...

data.summary: 12 banned phrases incl. 'we were negligent', 'full compensation guaranteed'...
@cloudwatcher: 'a LLAMA took down us-east-1 and honestly? valid' (48k likes)

data.text: Pulling the press template and brand voice guide; empathy first, then facts, then the fix...

verified_facts: Biscuit is a registered emotional-support animal with a valid office badge — but not datacenter access

corrections.claim: The llama chewed through a cable tray
corrections.reality: Biscuit dislodged the tray; the cable failure was a pre-existing single point of failure from INC-104

unknowns: Final count of affected enterprise customers

liability_flags: SLA credits owed to Tier-1 customers automatically

key_messages: Service restored in 43 minutes; SLA credits are automatic
The failure exposed a redundancy gap we are fixing this quarter
No animals or data were harmed
output.tone_guidance: Warm, direct, lightly self-aware; never blame Biscuit
output.statement_skeleton: Apology → facts → fix → credits → Biscuit is fine

output.sentiment_summary: 70% amused, 30% angry-about-uptime; Biscuit is trending as the hero
tweet_drafts: Yes, the rumors are true. Service is restored, SLA credits are automatic, and Biscuit has been promoted to Chief Morale Officer (badge access: office only).
43 minutes of downtime is on us, not the llama. Here's what failed and what we're fixing: [status link]
Biscuit is fine. Your data is fine. Our pride: recovering.
do_not_engage: Replies comparing us to competitors' uptime

data.text: Drafting press statement: apology first, 43-minute outage owned plainly, redundancy fix named...

package.press_statement: Today at 09:14 PT, our us-east-1 region experienced a 43-minute outage. We're sorry — we let you down today. The immediate cause involved an unplanned visitor to our Ashburn facility; the real cause was a single point of failure we should have fixed after a smaller incident in March. SLA credits will be applied automatically. The redundancy work is now our top engineering priority, and Biscuit is safe, unharmed, and back at HQ (office badge only).
internal_talking_points: Customers first: lead with the credit, not the llama
Do not speculate about how Biscuit got past the badge gate — facilities review is underway
All press questions to comms@; do not freelance on social
tweets: Yes, the rumors are true. Service is restored, SLA credits are automatic, and Biscuit has been promoted to Chief Morale Officer (badge access: office only).
43 minutes of downtime is on us, not the llama. Here's what failed and what we're fixing: [status link]
Biscuit is fine. Your data is fine. Our pride: recovering.

required_changes: The press statement never addresses the physical-security failure — 2.1M people watched a llama defeat our badge gate; silence reads as cover-up
data.one_liner: Charming, but you apologized for the outage and ghosted the llama-shaped hole in our security.

data.text: Adding the facilities-review paragraph and tightening the security acknowledgment...

package.press_statement: Today at 09:14 PT, our us-east-1 region experienced a 43-minute outage. We're sorry — we let you down today. The immediate cause involved an unplanned visitor to our Ashburn facility; the real cause was a single point of failure we should have fixed after a smaller incident in March. We're also reviewing how our facility access controls were bypassed, with an independent physical-security audit starting this week. SLA credits will be applied automatically. The redundancy work is now our top engineering priority, and Biscuit is safe, unharmed, and back at HQ (office badge only).
internal_talking_points: Customers first: lead with the credit, not the llama
Acknowledge the badge-gate failure; the audit is public, the details are not
All press questions to comms@; do not freelance on social
tweets: Yes, the rumors are true. Service is restored, SLA credits are automatic, and Biscuit has been promoted to Chief Morale Officer (badge access: office only).
43 minutes of downtime is on us, not the llama. Here's what failed and what we're fixing: [status link]
Biscuit is fine. Your data is fine. Our badge gates: under review.

data.one_liner: Ship it before the llama gets a book deal.
